questions.q: What is the term given to the use of banned substances by athletes to enhance their performance?
Write a short note on the effects of doping on an athlete's health.
questions.answer: Doping can lead to serious health problems, including liver damage, kidney failure, and increased risk of heart attack and stroke.

questions.q: Which of the following is a banned substance in sports?
questions.options: A) Steroids, B) Ephedrine, C) Caffeine, D) Creatine

questions.q: Explain the role of the World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) in preventing doping in sports.
questions.answer: WADA is responsible for setting and enforcing anti-doping rules, conducting research, and providing education and resources to athletes, coaches, and officials.

questions.q: True or False: Doping is only a problem in professional sports.
What is the name of the test used to detect the presence of banned substances in an athlete's urine sample?
Write a short note on the consequences of doping in sports, including both personal and professional repercussions.
questions.answer: Doping can result in penalties, including fines, suspensions, and loss of medals or titles. It can also damage an athlete's reputation and relationships with sponsors and fans.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a banned substance that can be used to enhance endurance?
questions.options: A) EPO, B) Human Growth Hormone (HGH), C) Steroids, D) Creatine

questions.q: Explain the concept of 'just cause' in relation to doping in sports.
questions.answer: Just cause refers to the idea that an athlete's doping offense must be intentional and not accidental. It means that the athlete must have knowingly or recklessly used a banned substance.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The use of banned substances by athletes to enhance their performance is known as _______________________.
